content_markdown:
The diagram shows an energy balance climate model for a planet.

The intensities of the reflected and radiated radiation are given in terms of the incident intensity $I$. Which of the following is the albedo of this planet?

diagram_metadata.description: An energy balance diagram showing incoming intensity I, reflected from atmosphere 0.25I, reflected by planet surface 0.15I, and radiated by planet surface 0.60I.

vibe_explanation: Albedo is defined as the ratio of the total reflected power (or intensity) to the total incident power (or intensity). In this model, radiation is reflected by both the atmosphere ($0.25I$) and the planet surface ($0.15I$). Therefore, the total reflected intensity is $0.25I + 0.15I = 0.40I$. Dividing this by the incident intensity $I$ gives an albedo of 0.40. Note that the 'radiated' component ($0.60I$) is emitted thermal radiation, not reflected radiation, so it is excluded from the albedo calculation.
